framework.explanation: Risk level reflects how cautious the roadmap should be based on current errors and recent trend. Readiness level reflects whether the student should reinforce, improve, or move to a next concept.

reasoning.content: The student is improving compared with the previous review {ref_review_previous}, but the latest review still shows unstable input and output handling {ref_review_current}. The previous attempt code also shows raw input handling that likely caused the issue {ref_code_previous}.

refs.content: Previous review: the main issue was treating numeric input as raw strings.
Current review: input handling improved, but the final result is still unstable.

roadmap_summary.content: Start with a closely related exercise to stabilize the same concept {ref_exercise_step_1}, then broaden practice while staying near the current topic {ref_exercise_step_2}.

refs.content: Sum Three Numbers: Read three integers and print their sum.
Rectangle Perimeter: Read width and height, then print the perimeter.

exercise.title: Sum Three Numbers
exercise.description: Read three integers and print their sum.
exercise.content: Write a program that reads three integers and prints their sum.
exercise.directive: Step 1: Practice the same core skill in a very similar task first.

exercise.title: Rectangle Perimeter
exercise.description: Read width and height, then print the perimeter.
exercise.content: Write a program that reads width and height and prints the perimeter.
exercise.directive: Step 2: Broaden the same skill in a slightly less familiar exercise.
